comments.post_body:
Celebrating 5 Years at Microsoft 

If you had told me when I graduated from WVU in 2014 with a Bachelor of Arts in Criminology that I would one day be a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft with a Master of Science in Software Engineering, I might not have believed you. Yet here I am in 2025, reflecting on an incredible journey filled with growth, opportunity, and gratitude.

First, I owe so much to GEICO’s Veterans IT Development Program, which gave me my start in programming and changed the trajectory of my career. A heartfelt thanks also to Deloitte, where I learned invaluable soft skills and the true power of an inclusive and collaborative organization.

I’m deeply grateful to the West Virginia Army National Guard for enabling me to earn my master’s degree debt-free through their exceptional tuition assistance programs. If you’re looking for the best deal in education, this is it!

My time as a commissioned Field Artillery Officer in the Army National Guard has taught me leadership and resilience in ways that no classroom or corporate training ever could.

To all the incredible civilian and military leaders and mentors I’ve had the privilege of learning from over the years—your guidance and support have truly shaped who I am today. Your impact on my life has been profound, and I am forever grateful.

This journey is a testament to the power of hard work and having clear goals. It truly is the American dream.

I’m incredibly thankful, humbled, and blessed to be where I am today. Here’s to the next chapter!

#Microsoft #SoftwareEngineering #ArmyNationalGuard #Gratitude #AmericanDream
comments.context_summary: The commenter is celebrating their 5-year anniversary at Microsoft, reflecting on their journey from graduating with a degree in Criminology to becoming a Senior Software Engineer, and expressing gratitude for the opportunities and mentors that have shaped their career. They attribute their success to hard work, clear goals, and the support of various organizations, including the Army National Guard and previous employers.

Exciting News!

I’m happy to share that I’ve joined Noble as a Federal and State/Local Sales Representative! I’m looking forward to this new opportunity and the chance to work with great people, providing solutions that make an impact.

If we’ve worked together before or if you’re looking for the right products and support, let’s connect! Feel free to reach out for my contact information—I’d love to chat and see how I can help.

Looking forward to what’s ahead!

comments.post_body:
Change is Coming: The Army Fitness Test Evolves

Starting June 1, 2025, the Army is implementing the new Army Fitness Test (AFT) - a five-event assessment built to meet the demands of modern warfighting.

This isn’t just about removing the standing power throw or renaming the ACFT. It’s about raising the bar for readiness, lethality, and shared standards.

Key changes:
• AFT becomes the official test of record
• 5 events: Deadlift, Push-Up, Sprint-Drag-Carry, Plank, 2-Mile Run
• Sex-neutral, age-normed standards for 21 combat MOSs
• Min score: 350 total / 60 per event (combat MOSs)
• Min score: 300 total (combat-support MOSs)

This change reinforces our commitment to excellence - regardless of rank, MOS, or gender.

We lead with standards.

Are you ready to meet the challenge?

#ArmyFitnessTest #WarfighterStandards #AJOC #ArmyStrong #JuniorOfficers #CombatReady #WarriorEthos #Lethality #Readiness 

The Army Leader Exchange  The Center for Junior Officers Kris Saling  US Army
comments.context_summary: The US Army is introducing a new Army Fitness Test (AFT) starting June 1, 2025, which includes five events and aims to raise the bar for readiness and lethality with sex-neutral and age-normed standards. The commenter is responding to the announcement of this change and the Army's commitment to excellence and higher standards.

comments.post_body: It has been a long journey since I took my first step with Microsoft 20 years ago – a Visual Basic support call that came my way just a few months out of college. I have learned a lot since then, and I have been fortunate to travel the world, build a family, and through it all continue solving hard problems alongside great colleagues here at Microsoft. I’m excited to take another step on this journey today as Vice President of Mission Engineering at Microsoft! Thanks to everyone who has partnered with me along the way. I am humbled by the trust that has been placed in me and I'm looking forward to what’s next.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new role as Vice President of Mission Engineering at Microsoft, responding to the author's post about their 20-year journey with the company. The author is expressing gratitude and humility as they take on this new step in their career.

comments.post_body:
I was going to wait to post about the next steps in my career, but I am too excited! After graduating this May, I will be working for Booz Allen Hamilton as a Supply Chain Logistics Consultant! 

It has been a very long road to get here, and happy to have my hard work finally pay off. From being a college drop-out 5 years ago, to joining the U.S. Army and working every odd job in between, I gathered myself to get back on track.

Thank you to everyone who has mentored me along the way, I couldn’t have done it without you. I’m so grateful for this opportunity ahead!


 #career #army #supplychain #boozallen #wvu
comments.context_summary: The commenter is sharing their exciting news about landing a job as a Supply Chain Logistics Consultant at Booz Allen Hamilton after graduating, and expressing gratitude to those who have supported them along the way. They're reflecting on their challenging journey, from being a college drop-out to joining the Army, and how it ultimately led to this career opportunity.
